title: Red Sox win 7th straight game just hours after landing in New York
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox win seventh consecutive game, defeating the New York Mets 6-2 on Friday night.
Sonny Gray pitches six innings of one-run ball, leading Red Sox to victory despite travel delays.
Anthony Seigler hits a two-run homer, contributing to Boston's offensive surge in their latest streak.

title: Christian Gonzalez responds to report that he isn’t physical enough for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez publicly addressed Greg Bedard's report on his physicality in the Patriots' secondary on social media.
Gonzalez's NFL performance includes 69 tackles in 2025, comparable to other top physical corners like Kamari Lassiter.
The Patriots secondary in 2026 features new additions, including veteran safety Kevin Byard and corner Marcus Jones.

title: Here’s who experts have the Red Sox selecting with the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox hold the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft, experts divided on their selection choice.
The Athletic predicts outfielder Chase Brunson from TCU for Boston's 20th pick in the MLB Draft.
Bleacher Report suggests Boston will choose Taylor Rabe, a right-handed pitcher from Ole Miss.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Little House on the Prairie' is now available on Netflix, featuring Crosby Fitzgerald, Alice Halsey, and Luke Bracey.
'The Long Walk', a Stephen King adaptation, and 'Project Hail Mary' starring Ryan Gosling are streaming on HBO Max and Prime Video.
'The Five-Star Weekend', a series starring Jennifer Garner, is available on Peacock, centered around a personal journey on Nantucket.

title: A 4th Red Sox player has been named a 2026 MLB All-Star
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ceddanne Rafaela of the Red Sox named to 2026 MLB All-Star Game, replacing injured Yankees outfielder Aaron Judge.
Rafaela, 25, is having a career year with a .283 average, 8 home runs, and 39 RBIs through 87 games.
Rafaela is the fourth Red Sox player selected to the All-Star Game, joining teammates Contreras, Suarez, and Chapman.

title: Joey Chestnut to compete in inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Joey Chestnut will compete in the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship on July 11 in Seabrook, New Hampshire.
Chestnut will face Geoff Esper, who holds the record of eating 281 wings in 10 minutes, at The Brook Casino.
The championship celebrates Saugus Wings from Kowloon, coated in a sticky, garlicky Asian-style barbecue sauce.

title: France-Morocco was a fitting end to a spectacular World Cup in Boston
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in the World Cup quarterfinal at Foxborough with goals from Mbappé and Dembélé.
Boston successfully hosted seven matches of the 2026 World Cup, drawing 63,811 fans for the France-Morocco game.
The event fostered international camaraderie, notably with Scottish and Norwegian fan celebrations in Boston.

title: Gloucester woman accused of plotting to kill childhood friend using WD-40
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Phyllis Palazzola of Gloucester faces charges for allegedly attacking friend Marianna DiMercurio with a WD-40-soaked rag.
DiMercurio claims Palazzola intended to kill them both by driving off a bridge, following a dispute over a $160,000 debt.
Palazzola was indicted on multiple charges, including attempted murder; arraignment set for July 21 in Essex Superior Court.

title: How a Waltham barber became France’s go-to during the World Cup
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Waltham barber Coco Fernandez became the trusted barber for France's national team during the World Cup.
Fernandez's involvement began with a WhatsApp message for a haircut at the Four Seasons Hotel in Boston.
Fernandez regularly cuts hair for French soccer stars Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé during their Boston stay.

title: Red Sox face lengthy travel issues ahead of series vs. Mets
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox faced a 17-hour travel delay from Chicago to New York due to plane issues.
The team's departure was delayed due to a broken tug and a cockpit light issue.
MLB delayed the Red Sox and Mets game start from 7:15 p.m. to 7:50 p.m. on Friday.
